TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy_Allan_Bristow, title = Allan_Bristow, year = 2025, month = May, day = 5, note = {In Wikipedia [Sakizaya]}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/wiki/Allan_Bristow} }

S.FORM: Allan Bristow nilacul u siwkay niza u etul niza sa, u tatama ciniza, nalecuhan ciniza i 1951 a mihcaan, 8 a bulad, 23 a demiad. u miunduay a kawaw niza, namimali i Amilika NBA. i 1973 a mihcaan, mapili' ciniza nu Feichen 79 tademaw. kakuniza kalunasulitan-sulu u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika Allan Mercer Bristow, Jr. is a retired American professional basketball player, coach, and executive. Bristow played college basketball at Virginia Tech, and was selected by the Philadelphia 76ers in the second round of the 1973 NBA draft. A 6 ft 7 in, 210 lb (95 kg) forward, he had a 10-year career in both the NBA and the ABA, playing for the Sixers, the San Antonio Spurs , the Utah Jazz, and finishing his playing career with the Dallas Mavericks. His nickname was "Disco".
S.PHON: ar~…ær~…æan bristow nir~…æa ¶ur~…æ u siwkaj niza u …ôtur~…æ niza sa, u tatama ¶iniza, nar~…æ…ô ¶uƒßan ¶iniza i **** a miƒß ¶aan, * a bur~…æad, ** a d…ômiad. u miunduaj a kawaw niza, namimar~…æi i amir~…æika nba. i **** a miƒß ¶aan, mapir~…æi î~ ° ¶iniza nu f…ôi ¶ƒß…ôn ** tad…ômaw. kakuniza kar~…æunasur~…æitan-sur~…æu u sur~…æit nu ƒßur~…æam atu amir~…æika ar~…ær~…æan m…ôr ¶…ôr bristow, *r. is a r…ôtir…ôd am…ôri ¶an prof…ôssionar~…æ bask…ôtbar~…ær~…æ pr~…æaj…ôr, ¶oa ¶ƒß, and …ô*…ô ¶uti*…ô. bristow pr~…æaj…ôd ¶or~…ær~…æ…ôg…ô bask…ôtbar~…ær~…æ at *irginia t…ô ¶ƒß, and was s…ôr~…æ…ô ¶t…ôd bj tƒß…ô pƒßir~…æad…ôr~…æpƒßia **…ôrs in tƒß…ô s…ô ¶ond round of tƒß…ô **** nba draft. a * ft * in, *** r~…æb (** kg) forward, ƒß…ô ƒßad a **-j…ôar ¶ar…ô…ôr in botƒß tƒß…ô nba and tƒß…ô aba, pr~…æaji≈ã for tƒß…ô si*…ôrs, tƒß…ô san antonio spurs , tƒß…ô utaƒß *azz, and finisƒßi≈ã ƒßis pr~…æaji≈ã ¶ar…ô…ôr witƒß tƒß…ô dar~…ær~…æas ma*…ôri ¶ks. ƒßis ni ¶knam…ô was "dis ¶o".
S.FORM: Allan Bristow nilacul u siwkay niza u etul niza sa, u tatama ciniza, nalecuhan ciniza i 1951 a mihcaan, 8 a bulad, 23 a demiad. u miunduay a kawaw niza, namimali i Amilika NBA. i 1973 a mihcaan, mapili' ciniza nu Feichen 79 tademaw. kakuniza kalunasulitan-sulu u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika Allan Mercer Bristow, Jr. is a retired American professional basketball player, coach, and executive. Bristow played college basketball at Virginia Tech, and was selected by the Philadelphia 76ers in the second round of the 1973 NBA draft. A 6 ft 7 in, 210 lb (95 kg) forward, he had a 10-year career in both the NBA and the ABA, playing for the Sixers, the San Antonio Spurs , the Utah Jazz, and finishing his playing career with the Dallas Mavericks. His nickname was "Disco".
